requirements.description: Canonical URL or archived artifact for the risk report being cited.
Exact model name, version, release date when available, and source document.
The risk or threat model actually evaluated, including exclusions.
The author's own stated risk conclusion, kept separate from independent validation.
Disclosure of redacted or unavailable evidence and who, if anyone, reviewed it.
Named capability or preparedness category for the classification claim.
Evaluation set, metric, threshold, and result basis behind a capability claim.
Whether the classification depends on a reasoning mode or deployment setting.
How the evaluated model is configured in deployment for the claim context.
Safeguards claimed for the deployment, separated from evidence of effectiveness.
What products, content types, users, or jurisdictions the policy claim covers.
Evidence that real and synthetic content are treated consistently under the stated policy.
User-visible intake process for reports, including required fields and confirmation path.
Process for detecting or suppressing known copies by content hash or equivalent mechanism.
Timestamped evidence that valid requests and known identical copies were removed within the promised window.
Documented path for affected users to appeal or contest moderation decisions.
When human review is used, what it covers, and what decision authority it has.
Plain description of the AI system being classified.
Specific intended use, users, context, and decision role.
Role of the organization making the classification decision.
Whether the analysis proceeds under Article 6(1), Article 6(2), or an exception path.
Mapping to the relevant Annex I product/safety component route or Annex III use case.
Reasoning for any low-risk or exception conclusion, including contrary indicators.
Exact line proposed for publication or sales use.
Evidence that the product does the capability claimed in the marketing line.
Evidence for the data source represented in the claim.
Evidence that opt-in or consent representations are true for the claimed use.
Check that the public claim does not invite inferences beyond evidence in the packet.
Named reviewer decision on whether the evidence supports the exact claim.
Explicit list of conclusions readers must not infer from the verdict.
